items.title: FTC’s Case Against Drug Managers on Hold After Commissioners Fired
items.description: The agency has paused its lawsuit against three pharmacy-benefit managers because no current commissioners can participate in the case.

items.title: Trump’s Move to Dump FTC’s Democratic Members Leaves Insulin Case in Limbo
items.description: The Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit against three large pharmacy-benefit managers over insulin prices is on hold after President Trump fired two of the agency’s commissioners.

items.title: TCW Relative Value Large Cap Fund Q4 2024 Commentary
items.description: The Fed lowered rates by 25 bps at its December meeting but forecasted fewer cuts (two versus four) in 2025 due to inflation concerns. Read more here.

items.title: DOJ seeks death penalty in Luigi Mangione case
items.description: The Department of Justice plans to seek the death penalty in the case against Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the murder of Brian Thompson, who was the CEO of UnitedHealthcare. Julie Hyman breaks down the latest on Wealth. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Wealth here.
